remember: Students will be able to recall the definition of statistics and its importance in economics.
Students will be able to explain the difference between descriptive and inferential statistics.
Students will be able to list the steps involved in collecting and organizing data.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of population, sample, and sampling methods.
Students will be able to explain the concept of frequency distribution and its types.
Students will be able to understand the concept of measures of central tendency and its importance.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of mean, median, and mode to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to calculate and interpret the measures of central tendency.
Students will be able to apply the concept of range and interquartile range to analyze data.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the advantages and disadvantages of different sampling methods.
Students will be able to evaluate the effect of outliers on measures of central tendency.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between different variables using scatter plots.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the concept of statistics and its importance in economics.
To differentiate between descriptive and inferential statistics.
To learn various steps involved in collecting and organizing data.
board_exam_objectives: To calculate and interpret the measures of central tendency.
To apply the concept of mean, median, and mode to real-life scenarios.
To evaluate the effect of outliers on measures of central tendency.
